methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in AI & Software. Range: $1.0B - $300.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in AI & Compute

private.name: OpenAI
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Seed), post-money $300B+

private.name: Thinking Machines Lab
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Seed), post-money $12B

private.name: Sakana AI
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Seed), post-money $1B+

data.narrative:
FlexAI, a seed-stage AI & Software company founded by Brijesh Tripathi and Dali Kilani, has raised €28.5M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer set of three same-sector, same-stage comparable companies: OpenAI ($300.0B), Thinking Machines Lab ($12.0B), and Sakana AI ($1.0B). This universe captures the heterogeneity of seed-stage AI valuations, which span from $1.0B to $300.0B, reflecting significant dispersion in the sector at this stage.

The peer-median method yields an implied valuation of $12.0B based on the median of the three comparables. This represents the primary triangulation approach applied here, given the limited availability of alternative methods with sufficient data granularity for seed-stage AI companies. The broader comparable universe includes eight private companies and one public company (Palantir, ticker PLTR), providing contextual support for the range of valuations observed in this sector.

The triangulated valuation range is $10.2B (low) to $13.8B (high), centered on a $12.0B median. This analysis carries material uncertainty inherent in seed-stage valuation: the three-company peer set exhibits extreme dispersion (300x between min and max), and the absence of revenue-based or comparable-transaction methods limits conviction. Key uncertainties include FlexAI's relative product maturity, capital efficiency, and addressable market position within the peer cohort. The median estimate should be treated as directional rather than precise.
